text.title: Internal Linking Graph Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every internal-link graph spec must enforce. Hard rules; designed to prevent orphans, exact-match-anchor overload, hub bloat, and missing hub backlinks.

rule.statement: Each topic cluster has exactly 1 hub page. Multiple hub candidates for the same topic are forbidden — pick one and link the others as spokes.
rule.rationale: Split hubs dilute the cluster's link equity and confuse the crawler about which page should rank for the head term.

rule.statement: Each hub has ≥4 spokes (cluster member pages). Clusters with <4 spokes are merged into a sibling cluster or absorbed into the hub itself.

rationale.source: Cluster-sizing benchmarks for solo content sites

rule.rationale: Hubs with fewer than 4 spokes do not register as topical-authority signals to the crawler; the cluster reads as a one-off page.

rule.statement: Every spoke has ≥2 inbound internal links: 1 from the hub + ≥1 from a sibling spoke in the same cluster.
rule.rationale: Pages with only the hub as inbound source rank but do not transmit equity to siblings. ≥2 inbound creates lateral equity flow that lifts the whole cluster.

rule.statement: Zero orphan pages: every indexable page has ≥1 inbound internal link from within its assigned cluster.
rule.rationale: Orphan pages with search traffic are silent equity sinks — fix or de-index, never leave floating.

rule.statement: Each target URL has ≥3 distinct anchor-text surface forms across its inbound internal links. Exact-match-only anchors are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Exact-match-only anchors trigger over-optimisation flags and look algorithmic; diversified anchors read as natural.

rule.statement: Every spoke contains an explicit outbound link to its cluster's hub. The link appears in the body (not only in a sidebar / footer module).
rule.rationale: Body links carry more weight than chrome links; without an explicit hub link, the cluster lacks the upward equity flow that the topology depends on.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the 6-step procedure end-to-end and run the validator before publishing the audit and fix plan.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree run leaf for canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree skip leaf for non-applicable inputs.
